title: Brine Mineral Assessment and Pilot Extraction
entry_mechanism: KAUST-SWCC research programme transitioning to pilot mineral extraction operation
executive_summary:
Desalination brine — the hypersaline waste stream discharged by Saudi Arabia's desalination plants — contains concentrated dissolved minerals including lithium, potassium, magnesium, bromine, and uranium at economically interesting concentrations. Saudi Arabia discharges approximately 4.5 million cubic meters of concentrated brine per day — the world's largest brine discharge — making it the world's most accessible concentration of dissolved mineral wealth.

Node DT-4 targets the systematic assessment and pilot extraction of commercially valuable minerals from Saudi desalination brine, beginning with lithium (critical battery material), potassium (fertiliser), and magnesium (lightweight alloys). Saudi Arabia's brine mineral programme, if successful, transforms a waste disposal problem into a raw material supply chain — converting SWCC's brine discharge into a lithium supply that serves Saudi Arabia's electric vehicle battery ambitions (AU-12, Battery Thermal Management).

The global direct lithium extraction (DLE) market is pre-commercial but growing rapidly. Saudi Arabia's brine lithium resource — estimated at 800,000 tonnes per year in dissolved form across all brine streams — is potentially one of the world's largest accessible lithium resources if extraction economics prove viable.

companies.name: EnergySource Minerals
companies.rationale: EnergySource has proven DLE technology (ILiAD process) commercially deployed at the Salton Sea. Technology transfer to Saudi brine application.

companies.name: SLB (formerly Schlumberger)
companies.rationale: SLB has brine mineral extraction technology and active Saudi Arabia operations through Aramco relationships.
companies.contact: SLB VP New Energy, Saudi Arabia

companies.name: Saltworks Technologies
companies.rationale: Saltworks develops advanced brine concentration and mineral recovery technologies with commercial deployments.

companies.name: Veolia Water Technologies
companies.rationale: Veolia has brine management technologies and active Saudi Arabia water sector relationships.
companies.contact: Veolia VP Water Technologies, Middle East

companies.name: MGX Minerals (Pure Lithium)
companies.rationale: MGX has membrane-based lithium extraction from brines with commercial scale-up experience.

partners.name: KAUST Water Desalination and Reuse Centre
partners.current: Brine characterisation research; mineral recovery research; membrane materials research
partners.upgrade: KAUST-SWCC brine minerals programme; comprehensive brine mineral mapping across all Saudi desalination plants; DLE pilot at KAUST research facility

partners.name: Saudi Water Company (SWCC)
partners.current: Brine discharged as waste; limited minerals recovery
partners.upgrade: SWCC brine access agreement for mineral pilot; SWCC infrastructure investment for brine capture; co-investment in mineral pilot plant

partners.name: Ma'aden (Saudi Arabian Mining Company)
partners.current: Phosphate and aluminium mining; some rare earth assessment
partners.upgrade: Ma'aden brine minerals processing investment; lithium and potassium extraction commercialisation; Ma'aden Saudi battery materials supply chain

partners.name: Saudi Aramco Research Centre
partners.current: Brine injection for enhanced oil recovery; some brine chemistry research
partners.upgrade: Aramco brine mineral characterisation; EOR brine lithium content assessment; dual-use brine management and mineral extraction research
